title: krascheninnikovia lanata var. subspinosa
intro: Krascheninnikovia lanata var. subspinosa, also known as Russian thistle or creeping saltwort, is a shrub-like plant in the Amaranthaceae family native to the deserts of western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Krascheninnikovia lanata var. subspinosa include its ability to reduce blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and have antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
